title: ProtocolLab Measurement And Telemetry Contracts
purpose: Define normalized measurement and telemetry evidence contracts without defining runner, collector, SDK, or instrumentation implementations.
scope: This specification covers measurement profiles, telemetry bundles, samples, summaries, collector descriptors, comparability, optional implementation telemetry, optional adapter/test-executor export, and benchmark timing authority.
context: ProtocolLab supports conformance, regression, benchmarking, diagnostics, and soak-style runs. Participants need stable public evidence shapes while preserving implementation freedom.

requirements.title: Normalized telemetry bundle
requirements.statement: ProtocolLab telemetry bundles MUST use the normalized public telemetry bundle contract and MUST NOT require OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, qlog, JSON logs, binary traces, EventPipe, pcap, or any specific instrumentation backend.

requirements.title: Measurement profile vocabulary
requirements.statement: Measurement profile identifiers MUST be limited to smoke, diagnostic, regression, benchmark, and soak.

requirements.title: Profile claim constraint
requirements.statement: A measurement profile MUST constrain the claim level a result can support.

requirements.title: Benchmark timing authority
requirements.statement: Runner-observed request results MUST be the canonical evidence for benchmark timing unless a specific test-executor contract explicitly defines another timing authority.

requirements.title: Optional implementation telemetry
requirements.statement: Implementation-side telemetry MAY be included as auxiliary evidence, but missing implementation telemetry MUST NOT fail an otherwise valid conformance run unless the run plan explicitly requires that telemetry.

requirements.title: Benchmark provenance and load shape
requirements.statement: Benchmark telemetry MUST disclose measurement provenance, requested load shape, effective load shape when available, and warnings when overhead or environment uncertainty affects comparability.

requirements.title: Diagnostic evidence boundary
requirements.statement: Diagnostic profile telemetry MAY include high-overhead collectors and raw artifact references, but diagnostic evidence MUST NOT be treated as clean benchmark evidence.

requirements.title: Comparability as evidence statement
requirements.statement: Comparability classes MUST be evidence statements and MUST NOT be interpreted as numeric scores.

requirements.title: Producer source and scope separation
requirements.statement: Telemetry bundles MUST distinguish producer, source, and scope so runner, implementation, test-executor, load-generator, environment, protocol, artifact, and other observations remain separable.

requirements.title: External correlation is optional
requirements.statement: Telemetry bundles MAY preserve external correlation identifiers, but ProtocolLab MUST NOT make external telemetry identifiers canonical or required.

requirements.title: Telemetry cannot decide conformance
requirements.statement: A telemetry bundle MUST NOT claim, revise, or override conformance pass/fail status after the fact.

requirements.title: Validity before performance acceptance
requirements.statement: A performance measurement MUST be ineligible for comparison or ranking unless all required protocol, operation, payload, completion, failure, timeout, and component-identity validation checks pass.

requirements.title: Requested and effective load and saturation
requirements.statement: Performance evidence MUST preserve requested and effective connections, concurrency, streams or outstanding operations, rate or count, duration, warmup, repetitions, timeouts, and target and load-generator saturation state when available.

requirements.title: Exact stack comparison compatibility
requirements.statement: Matched performance comparisons MUST require compatible scenario, semantic template, payload, exact protocol stack and binding, execution variant, load, network profile, implementation role, platform, package provenance, topology, validation, telemetry, saturation, and repetition policy.

requirements.title: Adapter optional telemetry discovery
requirements.statement: Adapter manifests and health responses MAY advertise telemetry export capability, but adapter telemetry export MUST remain optional unless a run plan explicitly requires it.

requirements.title: Test executor optional telemetry discovery
requirements.statement: Test-executor manifests and health responses MAY advertise telemetry export capability, but test-executor telemetry export MUST remain optional unless a run plan explicitly requires it.

requirements.title: Report claim level from measurement profile
requirements.statement: Public reports SHOULD state measurement profile and comparability limits when presenting measurement evidence.

requirements.title: Canonical protocol metric semantics
requirements.statement: Typed protocol-family scenarios MUST resolve every required metric to the Protocol Metric Catalog v2, which fixes metric identity, kind, unit, aggregation, measured window, and timing authority.

requirements.title: Normalized protocol execution result
requirements.statement: Executing typed protocol-family tests MUST emit a Protocol Execution Result v2 that binds run, cell, repetition, scenario, load profile, packages, selected component, endpoint, requested and observed protocol, required check outcomes, canonical metrics, artifacts, and family-specific proof.
